title: veronica rosea var. atlantica
intro: Veronica rosea var. atlantica, also known as the Atlantic speedwell, is a variety of flowering plant in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae), native to the mountainous regions of the Atlantic coast of Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been traditionally used to treat a variety of ailments, including wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The Atlantic speedwell has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
